title: QAOA Parameter Transfer for Hypergraphs
authors: Lucas T. Braydwood, Phillip C. Lotshaw
abstract: Variational Quantum Algorithms, including the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA), have shown promise in solving optimization problems but rely on costly variational loops that can themselves be hard optimization problems. Many methods have been proposed to mitigate this variational cost, with one of the most common being parameter transfer and concentration where variational parameters for one problem instance or for an average over problem instances can be used as a good set of parameters for another instance. Methods exist for reweighting these parameters based off graph degree and edge weights, but there has been little work on how to do this reweighting to handle higher locality problems where the graph structure turns into a hypergraph structure. In this paper, we analytically derive parameter reweighting rules to transfer parameters between different locality hypergraphs, resulting in a reweighting for the mixing terms in the Hamiltonian which have previously not been considered. These analytics rely on three cycle-free and low-circuit-depth assumptions, but numerics indicate that the results can be used even when these assumptions are not satisfied. The numerics obtain high quality results across a diverse set of hypergraphs with locality less than or equal to five, improving on previous relations that do not reweight the mixing terms.
summary: This paper addresses the computational bottleneck of the variational loop in QAOA by proposing a method to transfer optimized parameters from one problem instance to another. While previous work focused on reweighting cost Hamiltonian parameters, this work introduces a new analytical rule for reweighting the mixer Hamiltonian parameters when moving to hypergraphs. The proposed method significantly improves the approximation ratio for high-locality hypergraphs. The results remain robust even when the underlying assumption of acyclic structures is violated.
main_problem: The difficulty of the variational optimization loop in QAOA and the lack of parameter transfer/reweighting rules for higher-locality hypergraph structures.
main_result: The authors analytically derive new parameter reweighting rules for the mixer Hamiltonian (beta parameters) to transfer QAOA parameters between different hypergraph localities, significantly improving the approximation ratio.
method: Analytical derivation using a small-gamma expansion and a Berge-acyclic hypergraph assumption, supplemented by numerical simulations using the L-BFGS algorithm and bootstrapping.
model_or_system: The Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) applied to Max-k-XORSAT problems on hypergraphs, using a cost Hamiltonian with hyperedge weights and a standard transverse field mixer Hamiltonian.
key_observables: Approximation ratio (achieved energy divided by true ground state energy) and the expectation value of the cost function.
important_parameters: Variational parameters gamma and beta, circuit depth p, hyperedge locality k, and hyperedge weights w.
main_assumptions: The analytical derivation assumes Berge-acyclic hypergraphs (cycle-free), low circuit depth, and a small-gamma expansion.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates Berge cycles in hypergraphs; Figure 2 compares the approximation ratio across different QAOA methods showing improvement with beta reweighting; Figure 3 shows the performance for low-locality (k <= 2) hypergraphs.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the QAOA parameter transfer problem, defines the hypergraph model and Hamiltonian, provides the analytical derivation for beta reweighting, presents numerical validation across various hypergraph localities, and discusses limitations and future directions.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
